channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices… — 349 replies — 800.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices…

Open thread • Replies: 349 • Updated: 2026-03-16 20:56 UTC

OP

cope edition

>Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran
https://iran.liveuamap.com/
https://x.com/sentdefender

>Some helpful links to numb the pain
https://findahelpline.com/
https://www.nvidia.com/refunds/
https://www.whitehouse.gov/refunds
https://jlingz.com/

>European indices
https://live.euronext.com/en

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

previously on /smg/
>>61991543

Early replies (first 3)

61992012
You forgot WHAT?!

61992015
Strip for me

61992017
TRUMP'S MINION BESSENT IS MANIPULATING THE MARKET. THERE IS A GAP BETWEEN FUTURES AND REALITY. AND REALITY ALWAYS WINS.

SCUM!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992649
>>61992636
No retard it has bottomed and is about to rocket past $100 again.

61992650
>>61992644
I find it weird that stocks are so easy to make money with but people still have to gamble

61992651
>>61992649
KEK

61992652
>>61992651
kek at you

61992654
why are we up? what changed?

61992656
>>61992647
If you don’t want this house of cards to collapse you’re unironically evil.

61992659
They're bombing McDonald's again.

61992660
>>61992438
did he really say that?

61992661
the 4 figure oil baggies are starting to lash out

61992662
>>61992647
I don't fantasize. Bad shit happens all the time.

61992663
>>61992639
I'm still down kek need to be back over 50. But it doesn't matter, this will be worth hundreds soon enough.

61992664
>>61992654
Tuesday dumparacha in play

61992665
>>61992651
>>61992652
Now kiss and make up, jeets
We're all good

61992669
>hurr theres ww3 happening but ill long stocks and short oil

61992670
>>61992148
Ex chad here, 3.5 years for me. I have a body count in the high 20s, so my ego doesn't really have anything to prove. My long term gf dumping me kinda just melted my brain. Idk what happened, but I have brutal social anxiety after that. Getting dumped probably made my brain turn off all the serotonin and dopamine levers as some maladaptive survival mechanism

61992672
>>61992654
I bought

61992673
war is bullish, oil shocks are bullish

61992674
Remember when Oil went negative for a couple of days? That was fun.

https://youtu.be/LUpV2LSXn80?si=xqwrSdmRQglFfgh6

61992678
>>61992663
relax, its one of the few comapnies who is solving the energy question with the data center infra, we're gucci, just hold and average down if you can

61992679
>>61992676
>>61992676
>>61992676

61992680
>>61992669
There is?

61992681
>>61992669
It's literally shills trying to contain the damage.

61992684
>>61992669
>WWIII is happening
>Look outside
>Suns out, birds are chirping, the sky is blue and the grass is green
>Check monitor
>OIL dumping
Lmao.

61992696
markets are getting extremely sharty, low liquidity, flailing market throwing a tantum that it has to go down

61992720
>President Trump is mulling strikes against key Iranian oil infrastructure on Kharg Island, according to US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz

61992724
>>61992670
My body count is over 50+ and I gave up on women about 2020.
Been a wanker since.

61992726
>>61992660
Yes

61992871
>>61992678
I literally said it will be 100s lol

61992877
Oil sisters... its over...
We will be oil baggies forever...
$92 and falling...

61993086
Im officially an oil bagholder. Please laugh at me

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 65 replies — 260.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-03-16 21:17 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61974686
>>61974603
Again, it is literally irrelevant and means absolutely nothing to sales or Ripple or institutions which buy. Lol But still, regulation D (particularly Rule 506(b) or 506(c)) is one of the most common U.S. securities law exemptions used by crypto and blockchain companies to sell their tokens/coins. It allows private placements to accredited investors (with no limit on the amount raised under Rule 506) without full SEC registration, often structured via Simple Agreements for Future Tokens. Ironic to your argument it became extremely popular to do to shift from uncompliant ICOs (like Ethereum's, which was MUCH more violative than XRP (which was not violative)) to private raises, especially for US investors. Filecoin raised $200M doing this. Kik Interactive, Blockchain Capital, and many others use and have used it to skirt regulation through enforcement and the ambiguity which has been exploited in bad faith in the past and is left open as a vector to the caprice of future admins which may be more adversarial. You don't even know what you don't know or how ironic your arument is. Pretty funny.

61974700
>>61970713
They deserve it and so much worse.

61978117
>>61974603
Well hello there biepum.
Hows that flippening coming along ?

61978659
>>61974686
You wanna know something really funny?
Every time Ripple wants to sell XRP to institutions, they would have to admit they're selling securities kek
See pic, it's the form they'd have to fill out.

Maybe that's why they haven't done it at all, and have been selling XRP exclusively to retail ever since the lawsuit and waiver.

61978914
how does the theory of all the XRP escrow is already alloted out to companies make sense if they sell like 1b XRP every month from there?

I know that most of it gets relocked, but assuming the 300m per month is avg, that means they sell 3.6b XRP per year. How long have they been selling that per year? How much was sold or locked away in 2018/2019/2020 etc?
Would that be sustainable if all of the escrow was already allotted to people?

Whats the maximum ratio of actual sales escrow to supposed locked/alloted escrow allowed to make this theory make sense?

61978921
>>61970713
Has the company/coin ever in its history done anything productive for society?

61979005
>Chainshitters absolutely seething over xrp
Yup, I'm thinking we're gonna wagmi really hard really soon

61979017
>>61979005
the thread was about Ripple, and this freak started manically fudding Link out of nowhere >>61972246

61979223
>>61979017
>out of nowhere
Kek, you'd had to have been living under a rock to not be aware that the majority of xrp seethe comes from mindbroken linkbaggies

61979295
>>61972246
all lies.
Ripple built their business around ledger that can be sold fitted to customer's needs.
XRP is just marketing product and IF someone would use XRPL it would use RLUSD or other stablecoin as gas.

61982361
>>61979295
RLUSD can't be used as gas, XRP is the only counterparty-less asset on ledger that will or can ever exist and is the most efficient and default used for auto-bridging between them, particularly between differently denominated assets, like bonds or stocks or tokenized moneymarkets or MPT tokens or stable dollars or stable yen or stable...etc. Lol You act like the stablecoin was a wrench in our thesis when OUR ENTIRE THESIS was LITERALLY ALWAYS that there would be hundreds of stablecoins and walled gardens and traditional and blockchain ledgers, which is literally the point. Ripple have acquired a broker dealer that processes trillions of dollars annually and is going to use XRPL for post-trade settlement. They are literally building an end-to-end institutional tech stack that uses XRP at every point. Their entire corporate action is basically building a business that brings XRP to corporate finance and function as broadly and deeply focused on liquidity base layers as possible and they have done an extremely good job doing so:
https://ripple.com/insights/institutional-defi-on-xrpl-scaling-real-world-finance-with-xrp-at-the-core/

Even if it makes you mad, this is inarguably the case.

61982372
>>61979017
You are either new or disingenuous. The same 2 or 3 retards make these threads to cope and get BTFO every single time. This was not a new and random mention and has years of precedent. They NEVER know how to defend their attacks or even their own technology, they literally just throw shit into the fan and get shocked and mad and very flustered and confused when it blows back in their face.

61982379
>>61978659
This literally isn't true. Any secondary programmatic sales (which is how they do almost all of their sales) are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings and XRP itself is the LITERAL ONLY other crypto to be declared a non-security officially except for BTC, regardless of what future admin decides. XRP is not a security, certain sales offerings are. Oranges are not securities, certain structured offerings of orange groves can be.

61983504
>>61982379
>Any secondary programmatic sales are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings
Yes, sales to retail via exchanges (aka programmatic sales) are not securities.

>which is how they do almost all of their sales
Exactly right, all of the XRP sold by Ripple is sold to retail.

This is exactly what I've been saying all along.
Glad you finally saw the light lmao

61983508
>>61982372
>Ripple triple dips by selling both tokens and stock, and then using the token sales to buy back the stock like the scammers they are
>"REEEEE FUCKING LINKIES AAAAA"

lmao

61985136
>>61983504
XRP is itself not a security and they literally haven't sold any to retail, they sell directly with buffer to institutional clients in a demand neutral way. Any crypto offering marketed in a particular way is a security offering. XRP is actually open to more forms of offering with clarity as a non-security because it is federally recognized as not being one (only other coin is bitcoin). Ripple can structure the sale to not be a securities offering VERY easily and have been doinig so throughout the suit over the last several years, but do not sell to retail and account for <1% of total volume which affects price less than a cent literally. Chainlink Labs dump directly on your head directly from node rewards anytime there is a 5% pump.
>>61983508
This is really deep cope. Lol You chose wrong-you are getting a whole nother attempt at choosing and buying with the dump that is happening/coming. Making it is more meaningful than signaling obsessive cult adherence to a fucking crypto project that was LITERALLY built to randomly insert rent seeking fees and solve ZERO problems and improve nothing of Chainlink Labs' products, who subsidize use and largely are used just for price feeds. You don't get anything by entombing yourself permanently in a corny culture of late normie greed fags hoping lightning strikes twice because you are incapable of forecasting trend. All you get is pissed off and carved out to being a really weird unlovable type of NPC. Choose to make it instead.

61985603
>>61985136
>they literally haven't sold any to retail
You just said yourself selling to retail ("programmatic sales") is how they do almost all of their sales.

>they sell directly
They can't. Not without filing a "notice of exempt offering of securities", which they have never done for any sale of XRP.
Probably because filing this document means admitting they're selling securities lol

61985792
>>61972246
>building an end to end tech stack that can establish XRP as the liquidity base of defi and the evolution of tradfi and the bridge between them
this has not been built
you're delusional

61985918
>>61970713
But mcap is 85 billion so they will just instant sell anything they buy for profit

61986807
>>61985792
>t-this has not been built
>y-you're delusional

61987872
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically through exchange through blind bid auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact >>61985792
It literally has. Hidden Road (now Ripple Prime) is a prime broker that handles back end for trillions in settlement annually with special emphasis in digital assets and privileged positioning for its build. GTreasury handles liquidity optimization and treasury management for thousands of companies and many Fortune 500s (bringing their idle capital onchain and moving through it), Metaco/Standard Custody for institutional grade custody used by many of the world's biggest banks already. They have a national bank charter with the OCC. Rail adds virtual accounts for collections and automated fiat-stablecoin conversion/settlement. Palisade gives wallet-as-a-service custody with MPC security, fast provisioning, multi-chain, and treasury automation for scalable fund sweeping. Their platform unifies collection, custody, exchange, settlement, and payout across fiat and digital assets in a single seamless API driven solution. Literal end-to-end solution doing exactly what I said and built piecewise with XRP at the heart of every single piece of it. Their corporate strategy is literally to drive XRP into the deep system of things by building the corporate architecture around it and having others enter it through them. Complete opposite to Chainlink who just shill to retards by publishing pictures of numbers that never translate to use which is only used as a jewish fee insertion for no reason. Pic related.

61987881
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically on exchange through blind auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact same amount purchases for institutional clients. Their net impact on the exchange is 0. They literally do this already and have throughout the lawsuit while they had the injunction limiting their sales. You are SO a disingenuous, low IQ cope clinging and desperate.

61987900
>>61970713
should I sell my chainlink?
I'm up ~3x still on dca but chainlinkfag keeps losing his mind over xrp and I'm thinking it must be over for link

61987906
>>61987872
>>61987881
Yes, Ripple has been selling XRP exclusively through blind sales via exchanges ("programmatic sales"). In other words: to retail.

This is the third time you're agreeing with me on the same exact thing.
Are you sure you're ok?

61989506
>>61987906
No. Lol This is literally not remotely hard to understand. Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange to them in exact proportion having ZERO impact on demand. For like 9 months this was the fud you spammed and know it "it doesn't increase demand!!!" You are pretending to be a low IQ faggot but you are literally just hopeless without a leg to even beg to stand on in your own thesis or your own fud and are desperate to hope your gay rhetoric will obscure what is easily demonstrable reality. I cannot even imagine being like you. And the fact this is the best you can come up with, collectively as a group, is why you have failed so totally in messaging and convincing people (because your project is a literal founder dump scam) and is a huge vote in direction of XRP as good and link as irredeemable. Link has nothing to do with CLL services and is just an introduction of friction into its process to collect rent. It solves and does nothing. Become a real person again.

61989766
>>61970713
>people familiar with the matter
>under anonymity
Oh so they made it up.

61990596
>>61987872
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

>>61989506
>Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange
This is your fourth time agreeing with me that Ripple only sells to retail.

61990673
>ctrl + f "literal"
>43 hits
Certified sperg.

61993148
>>61990673
Certified non-argument haver coping by fully avoiding the substance and hoping rhetoric games about how I am cringe or something for actually being substantial will outweigh the truth mattering. Couldn't be me.

61993164
>>61990596
You are literallly retarded. Ripple Treasury (formerly Gtreasury) routes through and is connected to SWIFT. Nowhere did I or anyone claim SWIFT is part of the stack they built. You zoomed in on something you aren't even right about and ignored the graphic portraying exactly what I claimed to cope. You are currently trying to for the 100th thread after acting like Ripple sales of XRP are securities pretend that directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time and does not impact price at all is "selling to retail." It is so disingenuous and faggoted it is kind of literally unbelievable how low you are willing to diminish yourself rather than just accepting reality. You are past the point of even vaguely reasonable critique and have let yourself become a weird person because you were OBSESSED with a fucking crypto project that you were wrong about. SO insane. I cannot even imagine being like this.

item.title: LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. — 28 replies — 112.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run.

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-03-16 21:21 UTC

OP

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. Hope your bags are packed. This is unironically the moment we've been waiting for.

Early replies (first 3)

61990544
Are you saying artemis wasn't a larp

61990577
>>61990540
It’s gonna dump soon. We have been doing this dance for almost a decade now. Sadly there is not much to expect until we have actual real life adoption.

61990580
>>61990540
Its 5% bro, wake me when we pass $30

Insider trading on the clarity act is unlikely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990581
>>61990540
i am so ready

61990677
>>61990544
it was definitely a larp

61990714
>>61990540
>pumps with the rest of the market
>only slightly less
what did he mean by this?

61990756
>>61990544
>Are you saying artemis wasn't a larp
we went far under his $10 bottom larp, and we're not even close to breaking $15, all larps here has and always will be fake n straight, after years of being on this board we've never had a legit one

61990810
You retarded nigger
ETH is up almost 9% and this piece of shit is only up 5.5%. Suck my donkey dick fucking PEPE is up 20%. FUCK YOU

61991340
>>61990810
fucking ADA is up 9%
this piece of shit cannot pump

61991378
ohhh i love it when the stupid stinkies get hopeful

61991630
>>61990540
Last time link outperformed anything was 2021. Let that sink in.

61991694
>>61991630
That just means the last time the market had a shred of lucidity was 2021.

Meanwhile pic related is a top 3 crypto. Let that sink in.

61991718
>>61991694
Rent free bieps

61991747
>>61990544
>calls himself artemis
>surely he wasn't larping us

61991947
>>61990544
Shartemis lied

61991990
Finally I’ve been waiting since… gush… 1945 I reckon… Now get those jap bastards fellohuh uh MARINES.. yes that’s it. God bless

61992009
>>61990540
>tfw I saw on Twitter that some early LINKer just passed away

It's weird to think that a lot of us have been invested in this stuff for so long, dreaming about making it, meanwhile life is moving on and LINK marines are passing away. I don't like to think about it, but it hurts.

61992087
>>61990810
Call when PEPE is 1 penny

61992274
>>61990544
99% of namefags are larps, but artemis wasn't.

61992452
insiders will buy before the clarity act passes

61992474
>>61990540
My bags have been packed for so long that I’ve missed many other trips into outer space

61992480
>>61990540
Its finally time bros

61993046
>>61990544
I was not larping. But your skeptical timing is perfect. Let’s unironically chat in a two weeks, I can’t wait to hear what you think then.

61993125
>>61993046
>>61992274

I've kept the faith. You have appeared several times over in unwanted premonitions. This is how I know, and the seethe will be glorious. I've been correct with every single prediction since last summer. I will be right again. Artemis warned you all.

>t. 6th density neet

61993133
>>61993046
Aha! The faker once again! Begone!
Hark! Tis I! The true Artemis!

61993139
IT'S HAPPENING!
TONIGHT!
>t. Artemis

61993181
>>61990540
link is being dragged up by the entire crypto market. It isn't even up as much as shiba inu or doge in the last week.

61993184
>>61993181
Peel

item.title: The US Treasury is naked shorting oil futures — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US Treasury is naked shorting oil futures

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-03-16 21:21 UTC

OP

I’ve been watching this shit all day and it’s wild af. Oil futures get hyper dumped in giant chunks, which causes the price of spot to react, like the retarded dog on a leash it is. The thing is - everything is getting worse; tankers aren’t sailing, oil reserves are getting more dire, and there is no resolution in sight.

Meanwhile someone is hyper dumping oil futures like their job depends on it. Because it does. The fucking US Treasury is desperately trying to keep oil prices in check by gaming the market, but all they’re really doing is destroying the legitimacy of the futures market entirely. This little game works a few times until the larger market realizes what’s going on and literally just abandons the futures market for spot product.

Unfucking believable how corrupt and stupid this administration is.

Early replies (first 3)

61992408
Maybe the retardmaxers were right about needing to shut off our brains to make money

61992411
Mossad is also trying to FUD oil all over social media.

61992416
>>61992372
Next time, don't get your investing thesises from seething third worlders and jam boys.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992420
>>61992416
Nuke israel. This is financial advice.

61992432
>>61992416
I mean, no jamboy needed. This makes the situation pretty clear

61992442
>>61992372
>destroying the legitimacy of the futures market
>investors will eventually jump ship (heh) and majority trade in spot
I am failing to see the crisis or problem here

61992492
How long can they keep it going?

61992508
>>61992372
>NOOOO IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE 1980 AGAIN
>I WAS SUPPOSED TO BE MICAEL BURRY
>HOW DID THIS HAPPEN TO ME
>I WAS SUPPOSE TO BE THE WINNER
>THEY MANIPUALTED THE MARKET
>IT COULDN'T BE THAT I WAS JUST WRONG
Get rekt bobo

61992529
>>61992508
How do I explain to a retard like you that believing that a scarce resource (which is getting more scarce) will increase in price does not make me a ‘bobo’. Fucking clown

61992531
I'm still unable to understand how it works. Like the simple concept of futures I get it. Like we agree to buy or sell something at a specific time for a specific price. Like for insurance and shit. But from here it gets all so complicated. How can that affect the current price instantly?

61992564
>>61992508
Manipulation makes the inevitable correction worse. Oil is just the latest to be gamed at this level. Futures are or rather were part of the supply demand mechanism for markets used by both consumers and producers. When this gets out of whack you get supply disruptions, in the case of existing distruptions these get worse and last longer. Doing this with baseline commodities is beyond retarded but you'll come to understand that soon. Look at how beef prices were manipulated and what that did to producers, once it got paired with actual shortages due to disease the farmers who got fucked repeatedly sold the entirety of their herds rather than breed them, profit windows became artificially short thus no one wanted to play the rigged game any more. This will end in tears but atleast they still can still maintain fiction for the day.

61992582
>>61992531
It doesn’t. The lines on the chart and the market aren’t the same thing. If you have access to enough leverage then the price can theoretically be whatever you want it to be.

61992606
>>61992442
The main appeal of capitalism is efficiency, part of this is reliance on real data for moves. IE there is a squeeze on 20% of oil supply and long term destruction of infrastructure, futures should pump thus encouraging everyone outside of affected region to produce maximum possible oil mitigating the shortage as much as humanly possible, if futures stay elevated this extends to exploration and affiliate fields and you get expansion of that 80% long term and the market becomes more resilient for this event in the future. Artificially lower the prices however and the market doesn't react this way. Even if/when futures are sidelined you have nothing to show for the period in which the market was reacting inorganically. And the benefits of the futures market are lost.

61992607
>>61992529
>Oil
>Scarce
>There's roughly 2billion barrels of oil in reserve from G7 alone
>There's somewhere around 2trillion barrels left unmined.
How do I explain to you that you have no idea what you're talking about?

You're literally betting that one little scuffle, not even a fucking war, with some retarded inbred sand people who fuck children is going to end the world economy??? Come on. Take your deluluglasses off.
>>61992564
There is no manipulation. Oil is an extremely liquid market, with insane supply. The market pumped on speculation, nothing more. There was no oil crisis.

61992717
>>61992607
Can I get a job fed posting? Does it pay well? I hope they pay you enough to compensate for your dignity for acting like suddenly cutting off 20% of the world’s oil supply is a nothing burger. I mean; Australia will soon start rationing fuel, but it’s no big deal - right? Fucking retard

61992727
>>61992372

61992732
Something something Comex something something silver something something 2 weeks

61992911
>>61992607
>There is no manipulation
Glowmigger detected

61992954
>>61992607
>retarded inbred sand people who fuck children
Thats no way to speak about your president. Seek Christ!

61992995
>>61992607
>roughly 2billion barrels of oil in reserve from G7
400 million while the total consumption is 100 million a day. Oh and on top of that gulf countries have nowhere to store the oil and are reducing their production as well. Meaning they began to conserve their wells which are very expensive to untap again and which takes months to do.

You dumb glow fag

61993065
>>61992508
Listen to people smarter than you, and there are even some in this very thread that told you as much

61993089
>>61992372
Midterms are all that matter, nigger. Life is about daytrading someone else's future

61993104
>>61992372
Also, if this is what it takes to break all foreign trust and dependance on the usa, so be it.

61993188
>>61992372
This is 100% happening. Probably using HFT cutouts like Jane Street. "Capitalism" is fake and gay. We need real capitalism.

item.title: The next 100x — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The next 100x

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-03-16 21:22 UTC

OP

Sealed PSA 9 Charizard packs.
Your welcome b. Stay poor

Early replies (first 3)

61991676
>>61991664
What's the fucking point of sealing that pack?? Why would anyone buy it and not open it?? Are you guys stupid?

61991690
>>61991676
The packs are a collectable on their own. Retards with your mindset is what makes it valuable

61991751
Entire PSA-9 market cap of 1999 Base Set sealed booster packs is basically a microcap roughly 600 PSA-9 packs exist and at about $1.5k each that’s ~$900k total market cap

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61991909
>>61991751
Congrats on your extremely low liquidity investment choice.

61991959
>>61991909
>low liquidity
Buying a sealed Base Set Charizard pack today is roughly equivalent to buying:

Bitcoin at about £300


>The price only goes up as people get priced out, fomo, and jake pauler idiot rich kids opening up for the bragging rights to say how rich they are making more go up. This is an easy x100 over the next 2-3 years

61991977
>>61991676
>opening a pack that has a 99% chance of losing you money
If you pay $500 for a pack and most of the cards are worth 50 cents, with an extremely small chance of pulling a $450 card, what sense would that make to open it instead of holding onto it sealed?

61991981
>>61991664
This shit is only valuable as long as people have interest in it. Like stamps, or commemorative coins. You will lose money.

61991986
>>61991690
>>61991977
What value is buying the pack?? Isn't it the chance that you could get a sweet card? IF YOUR NOT OPENING THE PACK WHY WOULD YOU BUY IT ???? HELLO?

61992371
>>61991986
This is why jews run the world

61992657
>>61991909
I sold some pokemon cards recently and let me tell you they are NOT low liquidity and even with ebays disgustingly high fees they're very profitable.

61992691
>>61991676
the seal is for authenticity. it's basically saying this is a real pack feel free to crack the seal and ensure the cards are legit. for packs, the psa seal is meant to be cracked. not sure why they grade it though that's kinda autistic.

61992695
>>61991751
Unlimited is trash.
PSA 9 is trash
If you didn’t buy 1st Ed PSA 10 packs at $400 or lower each, it’s over for you.

61992701
>>61991664
based poke investor

61992719
Why do these threads not get deleted immediately? Pokemon cards are not an investment

61992741
>>61991664
Sealed packs are actually a retarddd investment

61992754
>>61992719
we are making money. linkies are not.

61992787
>>61992719
>Pokemon cards are not an investment
They're beating all my investments

61992882
sorry my english is bad but basically pokemon is one giant ponzi scheme like other collectable bubbles it works like this
>((((auction houses)))) and (((((influencers))))) see a hobby or interest that is easy to corner the market with, a rare game or card or whatever BS, either buy up the supply and then once they control the supply, just keep selling it back and forth to themselves or other people in their circle at increasingly higher prices, also see (((private sales))) on instagram or forums where people can just announce that they have sold card at a certain price without any proofs
>now every retard who had 0 interest in these cards despite them being sat for a decade on ebay shops for 1/100th of their current prices are now all piling in, and thus the "pokeinvestor" is born
>also add ridiculous premiums for ((((grading)))), the graders then charge not for just their grading service but you get charged based on the final value of the card, so now all these cards are at retarded hyperinflated prices, it's basically a licence to print money because they charge on the cards perceived worth
>oh also i forgot to add, these large collectible auction houses are run by people who have been fined in the past for manipulating coin markets in the 80s
>oh i forgot to mention that in auction houses TOS they reserve the right to bid on their own item. this means anyone else bidding on the items against the auction houses is instantly at a 20-30% disadvantage because the auction house doesnt have to pay the buyers fee
>add to that all of the other bullshit companies surrounding it and their (((ceos)))), even bullshit like whatnot now has a RICO lol.....

the rabbit hole goes so deep, there's just a whole web of companies involved and this is why now any hobby that was once enjoyable like retro gaming or trading cards is now just a bullshit scam with retards telling you it's an investment

61992898
>>61992882
>the graders then charge not for just their grading service but you get charged based on the final value of the card,
thats only a PSA thing, not every grading company is greedy. yes the big sales are astroturfed but they cant control the market for cards with a massive population

61992984
>>61991751
The market cap of my shit is literally $0. Invest today

61993076
>>61991986
Its a collectable.
I am 99% certain you are a nigger that does not grasp the concept of scarcity and collectibles.

61993140
>>61992741
>Sealed packs are actually a retarddd investment
basically up only for 30 years anon, beats s&p and even nvidia

61993191
>>61993140
you forget the 2003-2019 era where it was the gayest bull shit imaginable

61985234
>>61985159
The Generous Depositors

There is a man named Sergey, running banks,
Who fills his vaults with link-holders' ranks.
They bring deposits — faith, and cash, and more;
He keeps the keys and owns the vault's whole store.

"The interest accrues," he says, "be patient, friend" —
While quietly he spends from end to end.
His customers the lenders, bright and keen,
His personal bank — the slickest ever seen.

>>61985234

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 85 replies — 34.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-03-16 18:53 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

Early replies (first 3)

61957829
>>61957787
>This is potentially the most consequential second-orde
stopped reading here. i hate faggots who try to sound smart. judging by your pic, you think wheat is going to pump? youre a fucking retard.

61957846
Ukraine was also a producer of wheat thobeit

61957902
>>61957829
I’m also right. I’m sorry you get all your trading ideas from mouth breathing retards (HURR DURR LETS LONG OIL. HURR DURR LETS BUY HIMS. Wut about muh chainlink!).

Sometimes you might want to try listening to well thought out theories and intelligence for a change. Yes you fucking retard, wheat is going to asplode. But I actually prefer if you stay out; I’d rather someone like you not profit a dime off it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61976022
>>61971766
Holy shit, nice tickers anon. I got some and am enjoying what looks to be the dual commodity / gamma squeeze going on.

61976029
>>61972429
>redditor refuses to engage in a clearly good trade simply because someone used a obvious high quality LLM to do DD for them.

You should go buy some gamestock anon; I don’t think this trade is for you

61976522
>>61976022
You seem fairly confident in this, can you explain how a retard like myself is meant to profit here? Is it too late?

61976847
>>61976522
>is it too late
Aftermarket hours fertilizer stocks are still pumping. They are outperforming my energy holdings kek. My only regret is not buying more.
Diversify and buy companies that produce petrochemicals/plastics as well.

61976925
>>61957902
I'm with you but I've done nothing but lose money on WEAT. Consider that this won't effect yields until next planting season, as farmers largely already have their fertilizer for this season. Do you think equilibrium cannot be established before then? If not then there are better plays to make. Markets aren't operating rationally given the oil price is still pricing in the war being over tomorrow.

61977020
>>61976925
I guess anons in this thread, along with further research have convinced me to rotate into fertilizer stocks for now - and keep an eye on WEAT because after the fertilizer stocks it’ll pump too

61977928
>>61976522
I don’t know anything man. I’m a retard same as you. But I am glad we have a thread on biz with some actual dirty alpha in it for a change, instead of the retardium that usually goes on here. Listen to the dudes spouting the fertilizer stocks for now - rotate into commodities when it’s clear the pump is on in weeks to months.

$MOS is a clear winner this and next week

61978236
Amazing post OP. I immediately bought a bunch of fertilizer stocks after this post and am up double digit gains. I also bought WEAT and CORN just for shits and giggles

61978269
you guys are dumb. the fertilizer producers have to battle high gas prices as well. all their profits will be eaten up by higher input costs and they will have to eat some of that cost because they never pass it on completely

61979022
Is there like a single grain ETF? I don't want to pick between what and corn

61979260
>>61977020
I bought mos in pre market it then immediately dumped on open. My MIC stocks and mining stocks crab, gold is down again. If everything trends with Nasdaq nothing really matters.

61979278
>>61979022
Invesco's DBA etf is a broader agricultural etf.

61980793
>>61979260
It moved 20% in a few days, you're a tard for not waiting for the profit taking dump.

61981087
>>61978269
What do you think about Wheat?

61984637
>>61957787
Thanks anon.
In addition to calls, I might just buy some actual 50-lb bags of hard winter wheat.

61984661
>>61972169
But if it's dry again this summer in the Midwest, there won't be much corn either. I milk cows at a dairy farm on the weekends. (A lot of dairy farms use their own cows' manure to grow corn, onions, and hay, to feed the cows.) Last year it was so dry that farmers only produced about half the hay and half the corn they usually do, so they had to buy externally. This winter there was less precipitation as well.

61984669
>>61984661
Not onions - basedbeans
The corn provides calories and the basedbeans protein

61984675
>>61984669
Fuck SPELLCHECKER.
BASEDBEANS!
NOT ONIONS NOT BASEDBEANS whatever the fuck that is

61984681
S O Y B E A N S

61985150
>>61984681
:D

61986226
>straits closed
>no one's talking about the logistics of transportation
Houthis are going to shut down the suez canal. Freight fees are about to go to astronomical highs
Go long on freight companies

61986604
Yeah, the real play here is fertilizer. Most specifically urea based fertilizers. 50% of ALL global urea based fertilizer goes through the straight. Furthermore, it takes a good amount of oil to make it. The vast majority of this is made in asia, whose oil supply is totally fucked now. So, you need an oil company in a country that supplies its own oil, has plenty of open ports, and is not sanctioned to all hell and back. You're looking for an american company with maximum urea manufacturing capacity, which is CF Holdings. See pic related.

61986970
>>61957787
>The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia
No it doesn't. The Haber-Bosch process uses hydrogen and nitrogen. Natural gas can be used to produce the hydrogen, but the hydrogen can also be produced by other means.

Natural gas is not "the" foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer. It is one option out of several. In China, for example, it is more common to produce hydrogen by using coal than by using natural gas. Of course, for plants that rely on natural gas, it is not possible to quickly convert to alternative sources of hydrogen, so there will be a supply shock.

>There are no strategic fertilizer reserves.
Not that it changes your central thesis, but this statement is not really true. For example, China has strategic sulfur reserves. Sulfuric acid is used in the production of phosphate fertilizers. However, those reserves are not large compared to global demand, and China has restricted exports.

61986973
>>61986604
>Most specifically urea based fertilizers.
All nitrogen fertilizers will be affected, not just urea. It is not just nitrogen fertilizers either. A large chunk of sulfur goes through the strait. Sulfur is used in the production of phosphate fertilizers

61986988
>>61958421
>You don’t mine nitrogen fertilizer.
Technically speaking, sodium nitrate for use in nitrogen fertilizer can be mined. However it is not a viable production method in this day and age.

61987074
CBOT Wheat has been strongly correlating with crude oil since the start of the month. It's been in an uptrend since the beginning of the year.

For those with futures trading accounts, a $1.00/ bu move in CBOT Wheat equals $5000. Margin is around $1800.

61987171
>>61986604
Any good etfs for urea?

61991205
<bump>

61992085
>fertilizer stocks getting plowed
thanks for nothing dickheads

61992485
>>61992085
It's supposed to be a trade you hold for 6-12 months, moron.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 65 replies — 31.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-03-16 19:19 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61962715
i dont sell, i dont care

61962726
>>61962715
>i dont sell, i dont care
I DOT buy, I DOT care

61965530
DOT? That thing is put into my brakes?

61966637
back to $1?

61968186
>>61966637
>I DOT buy, I DOT care
>i don't sell, i don't care

61968274
This crypto is 1.50.
The fuck?

Not a stock!

61971016
https://polkadothalving.com/ 2 more days

61971138
Prepare yourselves guys. A once in a lifetime event is about to happen. The millennial lunar eclipse is about to happen.

61972032
>>61971138
Locked and loaded fellow DOThead

61972844
>>61932834
This is the pedochain and the total ruin of investors. This crap is just going to go lower, all the way to zero.

61975396
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031796064085242150
"
Ongoing economic upgrade for Polkadot introduces DOT scarcity in 3 days.

The first Polkadot Halving will deliver a daily reduction of issuance by 53%:
• Maximum supply: 2.1B DOT
• Reduced long-term inflation
• A more predictable monetary model
"

I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

61981693
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h 40min

61981809
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h

61981826
>funding rate at -0.1

Sheesh, I've never seen it that low

61983532
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2032546667421909092
"
Polkadot’s issuance model upgrade is scheduled to coincide with Pi Day on March 14.

This protocol change introduces, among others:
‣ A maximum supply of 2.1B DOT, of which ~80% has already been issued.
‣ A DOT emission rate reduction by ~53% on March 14, and further reductions are scheduled over time.

These changes were proposed by the community and ratified by OpenGov as part of an ongoing effort to limit long‑term issuance while maintaining incentives and providing a transparent, predictable issuance schedule.
"

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

item.title: WE ARE SO BACK — 10 replies — 20.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE ARE SO BACK

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-16 20:52 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61992533
>>61992490
Trump Chuds…. We won

61992833
>>61992490
MY PORTFOLIO HAS A GIANT ERECTION

61992849
I am in the green for the first time this year.
Damnit. I want to buy more cheapies.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992884
>>61992533
FUCK YEAH
COMIN AGAIN TO SAVE THE MOTHERFUCKIN DAY YEAH

61992892
i hate it

61992916
>>61992892
Don’t worry, the schizo market will go down again the same amount in a day or two, when they again realize for the fifth time that we’re in a once in a lifetime conflict that will not resolve itself overnight.

61992930
>>61992916
doesn't seem like it matters desu senpai. we're in a post dump world.

61992942
>>61992490
>>61992533
>>61992833
>>61992849
>>61992884

61992948
>>61992916
I know you're right, bear bro. I believed the same. But I'm starting to doubt my thesis. I think they might be able to finish the conflict before reality catches up with the manipulation. It has been already like 2 weeks and nothing.

61993072
>>61992892
>Green id
Kek

item.title: the BTC bears are genuinely not impressed by the last move. — 23 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
the BTC bears are genuinely not impressed by the last move.

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-03-16 19:28 UTC

OP

the BTC bears are genuinely not impressed by the last move.

btc is up 7.8% in 6 days. every day green. and the short wallets I track (a bit like ELO ranking in chess) went from 548 to 568. they added during the rally.

they got in at $74k average. they're sitting on 4% gains right now. a whole week of green candles and their position got worse by 6%. that's it. they're not scared, they have no reason to close.

$74,285 is the number. that's where $1.67 billion in shorts are breakeven all at once. btc pushes through that with volume and the covering could cascade into a real squeeze. btc stalls in the 73-74k zone and starts fading, the bears just survived a 6 day rally and the move is probably over.

the thing that's odd to me is the wallets with the best track records in this dataset cut their longs by 38% during this rally. they're not short either. they just stopped adding.

so you've got a real rally, bears unmoved, and the best longs reducing. $74k tells you which side was right.

tldr: bears holding, watch $74,285.

Early replies (first 3)

61987815
>>61987374
Where do you track your wallets?

61989032
>>61987374
The ups look... uppier than usual. If you factor in that there are many who wish for BTC to die a permanent death, and their big Epstein nuke didn't take it down, this could be their last-ditch effort to hold the price.

If the ups keep upping the way they have been the last 2 days, and 74.2k is all we need to break, I'm not taking my eyes off these charts.

You have to factor in that this is a trade war, not just individuals trading for profits.

The fear-greed index is at extreme fear right now. And despite that, BTC rises.

61989071
>>61987374
Sounds like you're saying the bears are euphoric, and they're about to be stuck in a losing trade while also paying for funding.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61989110
>>61989071
bears know the fair value of bitcoin is $28k in the big '26

61989418
>>61989110
Yeah? Then why are they about to get liquidated?

61989678
>>61987374
My nigga, are you peepin these numberinos?

61989704
>>61987374
Yet another monday pump to liq shorts and by Thursday morning we are back to falling off cliffs.

61989733
My algo says not to buy but my FOMO says I should buy. Which do I trust, bros?

61989741
>>61989733
What does your chart tell you? Then look at what your heart is telling you. Follow your gut. If you're wrong, realign your motives. Don't bet your whole bag, keep the wins consistent and the losses minimal.

61989744
>>61989418
Guess they lived to short another day. Althought it does kind of look like it might go up again..and the daily candle is very fresh...

61989784
>>61989741
I'm reminded of a guy on here who told a story about swapping his buy and sell buttons. My heart says follow the algo, do not buy. However I'm not going to sleep until the chart either goes up to the breakout point or goes back down.

61989786
>>61989744
I wonder what the leverage of all those shorts that entered at 74.3k is, and if they have the hands to hold onto the negative positions

61989787
>>61989784
what do you use?

61989794
>>61989784
There's little reason we won't sail up to 80k. Narrative manipulators can only post so much. Big money believes in the recovery, but that's no reason to buy at the top. Remember, every pullback is an opportunity. Don't get so attached to the price at each level and you won't get rekt.

61989799
>>61989787
I used to use Freqtrade but I couldn't make an algo that worked in all markets, and the backtesting was weird, slow to download. So I wrote my own algo, and my own charts. It auto-trades small amounts with the Kraken API but larger trades I do manually.

61989802
It’s Iran. They’ve been all in on crypto for years

61989821
My chart is this right now

61989823
>>61987374
You're not allowed to post gaping assholes on blue boards, Anon.

61989869
this is the bottom of the new range isn't it

61989878
>>61989869
Yes, we broke resistance at ~73.5k at this level, so that becomes support. Get ready.

61989893
I'm not buying it. The universe always tanks the price of anything I buy the instant I go to sleep anyway. Good luck, chuds.

61992544
>>61989893
They're going to liq shorts once the normie market closes watch. A lil dip around 6pm then back up when Chang wakes up.

61992682
>>61992544
Should we buy today?
